meta.title: Yalçın Haratoğlu — Frontend Developer | Energy & Enterprise SaaS
meta.description: Frontend Developer at Ratio Energy building Ratio EMS and RatioSIM B2B SaaS with React, TypeScript, Vite, and real-time WebSocket SCADA interfaces for the energy sector.

hero.name: Yalçın Haratoğlu
hero.description: I build production-grade B2B SaaS for the energy sector at Ratio Energy — from Ratio EMS operations platforms to RatioSIM analytics — using React, TypeScript, Vite, and WebSocket-driven SCADA experiences.

about.label: 01 — ABOUT
about.title: Energy Software
about.p1: I am a Frontend Developer focused on enterprise energy software at Ratio Energy, where I ship customer-facing products that operators and engineers rely on daily — including Ratio EMS and RatioSIM.
about.p2: My work spans complex dashboards, SCADA diagram editors, facility configuration tools, simulation result views, and trading workflows. I prioritize type-safe React architectures, predictable state management, and interfaces that stay responsive under high-frequency telemetry.
about.p3: I collaborate closely with backend, product, and domain experts to translate EMS and simulation requirements into maintainable UI modules, bilingual (TR/EN) experiences, and accessible patterns for long operational sessions.
experience: Own end-to-end frontend delivery for Ratio EMS modules: SCADA editor, facility editor, dashboards, mobile views, and trading screens.
Implement real-time monitoring with WebSocket streams, live charts, and resilient reconnection UX for SCADA and telemetry data.
Build RatioSIM user flows for simulation setup, execution feedback, and results exploration with React, TypeScript, and Vite.
Establish shared UI standards: component libraries, i18n, performance budgets, and code review practices across product teams.
about.priorRoles: Previously: freelance and contract frontend work for dashboards, marketing sites, and internal tools (including Eyeds and other client projects).

skills.label: 02 — EXPERTISE
skills.title: Skills &
skills.subtitle: Stack and practices I use to deliver reliable energy-sector SaaS interfaces

ratio-ems.title: Ratio EMS
ratio-ems.subtitle: Enterprise Energy Management Platform
ratio-ems.overview: Ratio EMS is a multi-tenant B2B SaaS used by energy teams to supervise assets, respond to alarms, and configure operational views. The frontend coordinates authentication, navigation, and module-specific workspaces under strict role permissions.
ratio-ems.role: Frontend Developer responsible for core application shell, shared components, real-time views, and cross-module UX consistency.
highlights: Structured the React + TypeScript codebase around feature modules and shared design tokens.
Integrated WebSocket channels for live SCADA and alarm updates with graceful degradation.
Delivered bilingual TR/EN interfaces with scalable i18n patterns.
Optimized bundle size and render performance for data-heavy operator screens.
ratio-ems.outcome: A stable EMS client adopted by operators for daily monitoring, with faster feature delivery through reusable UI foundations.

ratio-sim.title: RatioSIM
ratio-sim.subtitle: Energy Simulation & Analytics
ratio-sim.overview: RatioSIM enables users to configure simulations, track runs, and analyze outputs. The UI emphasizes clarity for technical users while supporting executive-friendly summaries.
ratio-sim.role: Built simulation-facing screens, results navigation, and shared visualization components.
highlights: Implemented scenario setup wizards and run-status feedback with clear error surfacing.
Designed chart and table views for comparing simulation runs side by side.
Aligned API contracts with backend teams for paginated and filtered result sets.
Maintained Vite-based builds with fast local feedback for the product squad.
ratio-sim.outcome: Improved time-to-insight for simulation users and a cohesive product surface alongside Ratio EMS.

ratio-energy.title: Ratio Energy
ratio-energy.subtitle: Corporate Product Website
ratio-energy.overview: Public website presenting Ratio Energy’s mission, products, and contact paths for enterprise prospects.
ratio-energy.role: Contributed to frontend implementation, layout system, and content-driven pages.
highlights: Implemented responsive marketing sections with accessible typography and contrast.
Connected product CTAs to live Ratio EMS and RatioSIM destinations.
Kept performance and SEO basics in check for corporate discovery.
ratio-energy.outcome: A credible digital front door that reflects the enterprise nature of the underlying SaaS products.

eyeds.title: Eyeds
eyeds.subtitle: Client Web Presence
eyeds.overview: Corporate website project delivered as a contract engagement with emphasis on brand presentation and lead capture.
eyeds.role: Frontend developer for page templates, responsive layout, and deployment-ready builds.
highlights: Built reusable section components for services and contact.
Ensured mobile-first layouts and fast initial load.
Collaborated on content structure and visual hierarchy.
eyeds.outcome: Launched a maintainable marketing site that met the client’s positioning goals.

ems-scada-editor.title: EMS SCADA Editor
ems-scada-editor.subtitle: Diagram & Topology Builder
ems-scada-editor.overview: Specialized editor for creating SCADA diagrams with symbols, connections, and bindings to live tags.
ems-scada-editor.role: Led UI implementation for canvas interactions, tooling palettes, and persistence flows.
highlights: Developed drag-and-drop and selection models for diagram elements.
Synced symbol states with WebSocket telemetry for live visual feedback.
Handled large diagrams with virtualization and incremental rendering strategies.
Added undo-friendly editing patterns for operator confidence.
ems-scada-editor.outcome: Operators can author and maintain SCADA views in-product without external drawing tools.

ems-facility-editor.title: EMS Facility Editor
ems-facility-editor.subtitle: Site & Asset Configuration
ems-facility-editor.overview: Configuration module for plants, lines, devices, and metadata that downstream EMS features consume.
ems-facility-editor.role: Implemented complex forms, trees, and validation-heavy workflows in React + TypeScript.
highlights: Designed hierarchical navigation for facilities and assets.
Centralized validation schemas shared across create/edit flows.
Improved bulk-edit patterns for enterprise administrators.
Reduced user errors with inline guidance and guardrails.
ems-facility-editor.outcome: More reliable master data entry and fewer support issues from misconfigured assets.

ems-dashboard.title: EMS Operations Dashboard
ems-dashboard.subtitle: Monitoring & KPI Views
ems-dashboard.overview: Dashboard experience combining widgets, trends, and alarm summaries for control-room monitoring.
ems-dashboard.role: Built widget framework, chart integrations, and real-time refresh behavior.
highlights: Composable widget layout with user-specific persistence where supported.
Live charts fed by WebSocket streams with throttled updates.
Alarm panels with filtering, acknowledgment UX, and severity cues.
Dark/light theming consistent with the wider EMS shell.
ems-dashboard.outcome: Operators spend less time switching tools; critical signals surface in one consolidated view.

ems-mobile.title: EMS Mobile Experience
ems-mobile.subtitle: Field & On-the-Go Access
ems-mobile.overview: Mobile-optimized slices of EMS functionality for alerts and key metrics when users are away from desktop setups.
ems-mobile.role: Adapted EMS components for smaller viewports and touch interaction patterns.
highlights: Prioritized alert lists and summary cards for quick scanning.
Tuned touch targets and navigation for one-handed use.
Reused authentication and API layers from the main EMS client.
Validated performance on mid-tier mobile hardware.
ems-mobile.outcome: Extended operational awareness beyond the control room without a separate native app.

sim-results.title: Simulation Results
sim-results.subtitle: Scenario Comparison UI
sim-results.overview: Dedicated results area within RatioSIM for exploring tabular and visual outputs after simulation runs complete.
sim-results.role: Owned results listing, detail drill-downs, and export-friendly table views.
highlights: Built filterable grids for large result datasets.
Linked charts to selected runs and metrics with consistent color encoding.
Added export hooks for CSV-oriented workflows.
Clarified empty, loading, and failed-run states.
sim-results.outcome: Analysts compare scenarios faster with less manual data wrangling.

ems-trading.title: EMS Trading Module
ems-trading.subtitle: Market & Schedule Workflows
ems-trading.overview: Module supporting trading-related schedules, submissions, and review within the EMS ecosystem.
ems-trading.role: Implemented form-heavy workflows, tables, and status tracking aligned with backend trading services.
highlights: Modeled multi-step forms with validation and draft-friendly UX.
Surfaced audit-friendly status timelines for submissions.
Integrated role-based visibilities for sensitive trading actions.
Kept terminology consistent with domain experts’ language.
ems-trading.outcome: Trading teams interact with schedules and data through a governed UI instead of ad-hoc spreadsheets.

contact.label: 04 — CONTACT
contact.title: Let's Build Your
contact.subtitle: Open to full-time, contract, and consulting frontend roles — especially in energy, industrial SaaS, and real-time dashboards.
